description: git-sync-all already handles fetching, fast-forwarding, and reporting dirty/unpushed repos across 70 repos. Research compared it against reposcan, mgitstatus, gita, github/stale-repos, and others. Conclusion: keep our script as the foundation and add three missing features rather than replacing it with an external tool.
notes:
## Research Summary (2026-02-27)

Compared against these tools:
- reposcan (Go, active): JSON output, same checks as ours. Lateral move.
- multi-git-status/mgitstatus (Bash, 523 stars): Compact output, -e flag. Same capabilities.
- gita (Python, 1.8k stars): Pretty terminal UI, batch ops. Requires manual repo registration.
- github/stale-repos (GitHub Action, official, 195 stars): Finds inactive repos by org, includes visibility. Remote-only.
- ghq (Go, 3.5k stars): Organizes clone dirs. No status tracking.
- gitbatch (Go, 1.6k stars): TUI for batch ops. Unmaintained since 2021.
- myrepos/mr (Perl): Multi-VCS batch runner. Old but functional.
- GitDailies, Gitmore, GitRecap: Commercial SaaS. Not relevant.

Verdict: Our script already does the hard part. The gh CLI fills the gaps (visibility, stale branches) without adding new dependencies.
title: Upgrade git-sync-all with missing features

description: The daily report doesn't show whether repos are public or private on GitHub. Add a section using 'gh repo list USERNAME --json name,visibility,pushedAt --limit 500' to list all remote repos with their visibility status. This is a single API call, no per-repo looping needed. Could also flag repos that are public but maybe shouldn't be.
title: Add public/private repo visibility to report

description: Currently the script shows unpushed branches but doesn't flag branches that haven't been touched in weeks/months. Add a configurable threshold (e.g. 30 days) that flags branches whose last commit is older than the threshold. This catches forgotten feature branches. Can use 'git log -1 --format=%ci <branch>' for local branches, and 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches' for remote branch staleness.
title: Add stale branch detection by age

description: With two computers, there's no way to know if the other machine has unpushed work. No off-the-shelf tool handles this well. Approach: have each machine push its git-sync-report.md to a shared location (gist, shared repo, or cloud sync folder), then add a section to the report that diffs against the other machine's report. Could use 'gh gist create/edit' to push reports, then 'gh gist view' to pull the other machine's report. Need a machine identifier in each report.
title: Add cross-machine sync awareness
